title: Symphysia floccosa
intro: Symphysia floccosa, also known as S. floribunda, is a plant species within the family Combretaceae, commonly referred to as the Raisin tree or Combretum. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of S. floccosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, S. floccosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various tinctures and extracts for its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Symphysia floccosa is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the madder family or coffee family. Symphysia floccosa is a member of the genus Symphysia, which is a genus of flowering plants in the Rubiaceae family. The genus Symphysia is native to tropical regions, particularly in Central and South America. As a member of this genus, Symphysia floccosa shares characteristics with other flowering plants in the Rubiaceae family.
